page: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8), alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography (AES, VAES, SHA_NI). In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, achieving a Geekbench single-core score of 2137.0 and a gzip compression speed of 34.06 GB/sec. Text processing performance is highly efficient, registering in the top tier at 2929.0 points. Multi-core and database operations, such as Redis SET operations at 15.37 million operations per second, consistently perform within average ranges.

As a memory-optimized instance, the r8i-flex.2xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a cost-effective choice for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. However, network bandwidth is limited to a baseline of 2 Gbps, and text generation for medium-sized language models (7B) is relatively weak at 8.5 tokens per second. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring high DDR5 memory speeds without the need for local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR5 RAM running at 7 GHz. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, it supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX for accelerated vector and matrix operations. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression, while multi-core metrics and database operations align with average tiers. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications. It is highly suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual instance featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel Xeon matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
Supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instruction sets
Strong single-core and text processing benchmark performance
No local storage and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for database operations and memory-intensive workloads
